2603.22620.title: Causal Discovery in Action: Learning Chain-Reaction Mechanisms from Interventions
2603.22620.paper_summary: Causal discovery is challenging in general dynamical systems because, without strong structural assumptions, the underlying causal graph may not be identifiable even from interventional data. However, many real-world systems exhibit directional, cascade-like structure, in which components activate sequentially and upstream failures suppress downstream effects. We study causal discovery in such chain-reaction systems and show that the causal structure is uniquely identifiable from blocking interventions that prevent individual components from activating. We propose a minimal estimator with finite-sample guarantees, achieving exponential error decay and logarithmic sample complexity. Experiments on synthetic models and diverse chain-reaction environments demonstrate reliable recovery from a few interventions, while observational heuristics fail in regimes with delayed or overlapping causal effects.
2603.22620.paper_summary_zh: 因果發現一般在動態系統中具有挑戰性，因為在沒有強結構假設的情況下，即使從干預數據中也可能無法識別潛在的因果圖。然而，許多現實世界系統顯示出方向性、級聯式結構，其中組件依次激活，上游故障抑制下游效應。我們研究這種鏈式反應系統中的因果發現，並表明因果結構可以從阻止單個組件激活的阻塞干預中唯一識別。我們提出了一種具有有限樣本保證的最小估計器，實現指數誤差衰減和對數樣本複雜度。在合成模型和多樣的鏈式反應環境中的實驗顯示，從少量干預中可靠地恢復，而觀察性啟發式在延遲或重疊因果效應的情況下失敗。
2603.22620.author: Panayiotis Panayiotou et.al.
